title: Dog Nesting Behavior and False Pregnancy: Symptoms and Triage Guide
meta_desc: Understand why your dog is nesting without being pregnant. Learn about pseudopregnancy symptoms, urgency levels, and how to help your pet.
content_html:
TL;DR: False pregnancy is a non-emergency hormonal condition in unspayed dogs that usually resolves on its own within three weeks, though you should monitor for signs of infection like mastitis or pyometra.

What is false pregnancy in dogs and why does it happen?

False pregnancy, medically known as pseudopregnancy or pseudocyesis, is a common condition where an unspayed female dog exhibits signs of pregnancy despite not being pregnant. This occurs because the hormones produced after a heat cycle trick the body into thinking it is preparing for puppies. Behaviors include nesting, mothering inanimate objects like toys, and even physical changes like mammary gland enlargement.

Is my dog's nesting behavior an emergency and what symptoms should I monitor?

The urgency level for nesting behavior associated with false pregnancy is Low, as most cases resolve within two to three weeks. However, you should monitor your dog for the following signs which require immediate veterinary attention:

  • Signs of mastitis (infection of the mammary glands)
  • Signs of pyometra (a serious uterine infection)
  • Lethargy
  • Refusal to eat

How does sending a photo of my dog help the triage process?

Providing a photo of your dog's mammary glands or their nesting environment can be very helpful for a triage professional. A clear image allows us to:

  • Check for signs of inflammation or skin irritation
  • Identify significant swelling that might suggest a more serious underlying issue
  • Determine if your dog needs a routine check-up or more urgent care

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

False pregnancy (pseudopregnancy, pseudocyesis) is common in bitches at the end of diestrus and is characterized by mammary gland hyperplasia, lactation, and behavioral changes such as nesting, "mothering" inanimate objects, and decreased appetite. It is important to rule out true pregnancy via history, abdominal palpation, radiographs, and ultrasonography. The condition typically resolves spontaneously in 1–3 weeks and requires no treatment. For mammary gland discomfort, alternating cold and warm compresses or an elastic abdominal bandage may provide relief; owners should be advised against milking the mammary glands, as this stimulates lactogenesis. For significant behavioral changes, tranquilizers (e.g., diazepam) may be considered.

Chapter: Reproduction

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 1395)

faqs.question: How long does a false pregnancy last?
faqs.answer: Symptoms typically last between one to three weeks and usually resolve without medical intervention as hormones return to normal.

faqs.question: Should I discourage the nesting behavior?
faqs.answer: It is generally best to gently distract your dog and avoid encouraging the 'mothering' of toys, as this can sometimes prolong the hormonal state.

faqs.question: Can false pregnancy happen more than once?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs that experience one false pregnancy are likely to experience them again after future heat cycles unless they are spayed.
